segments.text: Some time later when the wheat was being harvested, Samson went to pay his wife a visit, taking with him a young goat as a present. {
I want to go to my wife in her bedroom,
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: he said when he arrived, [^48] but her father would not let him go in.
I thought you must totally hate her, so I gave her to your best man,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he told Samson.
But her younger sister is even more attractive—why don't you marry her instead?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This time I can't be blamed for the trouble I'm going to cause the Philistines,
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: Samson declared.
He went and caught three hundred foxes and tied their tails together, two by two.

He attached a torch to each of the tied tails and set them on fire. Then he let them loose in the grain fields of the Philistines, setting fire to all the grain, harvested and unharvested, as well as the vineyards and olive groves.
Who did this?
segments.speaker: The Philistines

segments.text: the Philistines asked.
It was Samson, the son-in-law of the man from Timnah,
segments.speaker: The Philistines

segments.text: they were told.
That man gave Samson's wife to Samson's best man.
segments.speaker: The Philistines

segments.text: So the Philistines went and burned her and her father to death.
Samson told them, {
If this is the way you're going to act, then I won't stop until I take my revenge on you!
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: He attacked them violently, [^49] killing them, and then left to go and live in a cave at the rock of Etam.
So the Philistine army came and camped in Judah, drawn up for battle near Lehi.
The people of Judah asked, {
Why have you invaded us?
segments.speaker: The people of Judah

segments.text: We've come to capture Samson, to do to him what he's done to us!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied.
Three thousand men of Judah went to the cave at the rock of Etam and asked Samson, {
Don't you understand that the Philistines rule over us? What do you think you're doing to us?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I only did what they did to me,
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: he replied.
Well, we've come to take you prisoner and hand you over to the Philistines,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they told him.
Just swear to me that you're not going to kill me yourselves,
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: Samson answered.
No, we won't,
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: they assured him.
We'll only tie you up and hand you over to the Philistines. We certainly aren't going to kill you!
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: They tied him using two new ropes and led him up from the rock.

When Samson got close to Lehi, the Philistines ran towards him, shouting at him. But the Spirit of the Lord swept over him, and the ropes tying his arms together became as weak as burnt flax, and his hands broke free.
He grabbed the fresh[^50] jawbone of a donkey, using it to kill a thousand Philistines.
Then Samson declared, {
With a donkey's jawbone I have piled the dead into heaps. With a donkey's jawbone I have killed a thousand men.
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: After Samson had finished his speech, he threw away the jawbone, and he named the place Hill of the Jawbone.
Samson was now extremely thirsty, and he called out to the Lord, saying, {
You have achieved this amazing victory[^51] through your servant, but now do I have to die of thirst and be captured by the heathen?
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: So God split open a rock seam in Lehi, and water came out of it. Samson drank and his strength returned—he felt much better. That's why he named it the Spring of the Caller, and it's still there in Lehi to this very day.
Samson led Israel as judge for twenty years during the time of the Philistines.
